Cross-complainant Fairmont Hotel Management, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication against cross-defendant No More Dirt, Inc. is continued to July 12, 2018 to give No More Dirt an opportunity to file a sur-reply memorandum in response to the evidence set forth in FHML's reply papers on the issue of standing. While FHML did not submit any evidence in its moving papers that it has standing to file a claim against No More Dirt under the indemnity provision as Fairmont Hotel Company - San Francisco, L.P.'s exclusive agent, the court has discretion to consider new evidence in reply papers on a motion for summary judgment, provided that the other party has notice and opportunity to respond. (Weiss v. Chevron, U.S.A, Inc. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1094, 1098; Plenger v. Alza Corp. (1992) 11 CalApp.4th 349, 362 n 8.) No More Dirt must file its sur-reply memorandum no later July 6, 2018 limited to the evidence FHML submitted in the reply papers.
